comment.body:
Very clear intraday posts. Couldn’t be any clearer. And no one’s more precise..heck, no one even comes close. And the fact that she actually breaks down her reasoning is very much appreciated. 
The whole point is that volatility looks like it’s slowly climbing from here thanks to mechanical flows and recession-concerns repricing. The market’s still rangebound. Shes highlighted a day-by-day approach and scaling into longer-term positions slowly. But she also says vol of vol is being kept artificially low, so dealers prevent vol squeezes if the "Fed backstab" happens.
That said, higher vol brings higher theta risk, so you’ve gotta scale in super cautiously, because even if the market drops 300 points in august, time decay will eat most of the premium. 
She already sees the spot pivot, but she also sees the market’s still ranging and could break out intraday. I’d add that the nasdaq’s waiting on an upside gap fill, plus we’ll likely get posts from Bessent and Trump next week, and ER season is also coming. Alma even wrote a post saying there’s no such thing as 100% bear or 100% bull

comment.body: Rising volatility will eventually cap the upside. The call wing would be a short call. So as IV increases dealers delta goes more negative causing reflexive buying (thats why we've been squeezing like this). However eventually as IV increases it starts to work against the position (IV rising on high delta starts to cap upside)
comment.raw_body: Rising volatility will eventually cap the upside. The call wing would be a short call. So as IV increases dealers delta goes more negative causing reflexive buying (thats why we've been squeezing like this). comment.body:
I’ve been meaning to ask you a question for a while. It relates to this. You said “Daily mean-reversion threshold levels, 
which outline a normal distribution for that trading day—i.e., what the market is “betting” on that day. These levels/zones tell you the probability with which the market is likely to revert to the mean. The so-called ‘Risk Level’ is a propriotary output. This is a level that, if broken by the price action, confirms and reinforces the existing momentum.”
If I’m trading in day 0DTE, what time frame should I look at? I typically use a 15 minute chart as my default. How would I factor that in?
comment.raw_body:
I’ve been meaning to ask you a question for a while. It relates to this. You said “Daily mean-reversion threshold levels, 
which outline a normal distribution for that trading day—i.e., what the market is “betting” on that day. These levels/zones tell you the probability with which the market is likely to revert to the mean. The so-called ‘Risk Level’ is a propriotary output. This is a level that, if broken by the price action, confirms and reinforces the existing momentum.”
If I’m trading in day 0DTE, what time frame should I look at? I typically use a 15 minute chart as my default. How would I factor that in?
